questions.question: Who are the target users and roles for the app?
questions.reason: Single-user vs team/enterprise and required roles (admin, owner, collaborator, guest) determine auth, data model, permissions, and collaboration features.
options: Multi-user teams with sharing and assignments
Enterprise with organizations, admin roles and SSO

questions.question: Which platforms do you need to support for the initial release?
questions.reason: Platform choice (web SPA, native mobile, PWA, desktop) directly impacts frontend framework, offline/storage options, push notifications, and deployment pipeline.

questions.question: Which features must the MVP include (pick all that apply or add others)?
questions.reason: Functional scope (CRUD, reminders, recurrence, subtasks, attachments, sharing, search, offline) determines schema, APIs, background jobs, and priority of engineering effort.

questions.question: What authentication and user account flows should be supported?
questions.reason: Auth choices (anonymous, email/password, OAuth providers, SSO) affect user onboarding, security requirements, third-party integrations, and session management.

questions.question: What are the expected data persistence and scale requirements? (e.g., per-user data size, estimated MAU, preference for SQL vs NoSQL vs local device DB)
questions.reason: Expected scale and storage model influence database selection (Postgres, Firestore, SQLite), schema design for tasks/projects, indexing/search strategy, and cost/ops considerations.

questions.question: Which notification channels and third‑party integrations are required at launch?
questions.reason: Push/email/SMS/calendar sync/webhooks require external services, background workers, permission flows, and affects architecture and cost.

questions.question: Do you need offline-first behavior and/or realtime collaboration, and what conflict-resolution model do you expect?
questions.reason: Offline sync and real-time collaboration (WebSockets, CRDTs, operational transforms) significantly affect client architecture, sync logic, and backend infrastructure.
options: Read-only offline (cached data) with manual refresh
Full offline-first with background sync and conflict resolution
Real-time collaborative editing with live presence and conflict resolution

questions.question: Are there security, privacy or compliance requirements to meet (e.g., encryption at rest, GDPR/CCPA, audit logs, SOC2)?
questions.reason: Compliance and security requirements drive encryption, logging, retention policies, data residency, and operational controls which affect architecture and cost.
